title: Making normal NGM
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"41ov","text":"C. elegans is maintained in the laboratory on Nematode Growth Medium (NGM) agar which has been aseptically poured into petri plates using a peristaltic pump. NGM (rather than no peptone NGM) may also be used for imaging plates when screening bacterial strains. steps: For pouring multi-well imaging plates: Dispensing agar into multiwell plates by Saul Moore
For pouring maintenance/imaging Petri dishes: Plate Pouring by Priota Islam
Book the autoclave via the notebook on top of the machine. It will take around 2 hours for a 500ml bottle and 2.5 hours for 1L or larger bottles.

Take clean bottles and measuring cylinders from the glass kitchen - you will also need one bottle for the autoclave probe, which should be the size of the largest bottle you are autoclaving. Get sterile water from the media room.
Measure out all the pre-autoclave reagents and add to the bottle. Use a new weighing boat and spatula for each reagent. Add water last and mix thoroughly.
Add equivalent amount of water to the empty probe bottle.

Make sure the lid of your media bottle is not screwed completely when placing it inside the autoclave machine. Label with autoclave tape (‘NGM agar Rm 5020’), and place over the lid to prevent it coming off in the autoclave.
Turn ON the autoclave and touch the screen
If 'Fill with water' is on the screen, fill up the autoclave with sterile water until it reaches the grill/'Water Level OK' appears on the screen.
Place the probe bottle inside the autoclave with the temperature probe inside.
Place the bottles in the autoclave and make sure that the cap is not screwed completely.
Check the waste flask is not too full
Close the door
(On the screen) LOCK --> START --> MEDIA 121X15 BALLAST --> Start
When autoclave is finished, UNLOCK the door
Remove the probe and media bottles using gloves (the bottles will be hot)
Leave the agar on a lab bench to cool to around 55°C, ie the bottle is cool enough to hold for a second with a gloved hand
Add the post autoclave reagents
Try not to shake the bottle too much while mixing to avoid air bubbles.
The agar needs to be warm to be poured without blocking the tubings, so try to pour as quickly as possible and if not poured immediately put the bottle on a waterbath set to 60C until being used.
creator: Bonnie Evans
